QF.id: Role of RTAs in Mutual Fund Transactions_27

question.text: TER (Total Expense Ratio) in a mutual fund is:

answer.text: Paid by the AMC from its own profits without impacting the fund's NAV
Charged to the scheme's AUM on a daily basis and reduces the NAV proportionately
Charged to investors separately as an annual fee debited from their bank accounts
A one-time charge deducted at the time of investment

explanation.text: **The Logic:** TER is charged to the scheme's AUM daily and is already reflected in the NAV. Investors don't pay it separately -- it is deducted from the fund's assets before calculating NAV, thus reducing the NAV marginally each day. **The Trap:** Many retail investors think TER is an additional charge. It is embedded in the NAV. The NAV you see is already net of TER. This is a critical distinction. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers TER extensively. Understanding that TER is deducted from AUM (not from investor accounts) is fundamental to Series II-B as well. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When discussing TER with clients, explain that 'the return you see is already after expenses.' A fund showing 12% return with 2% TER actually generated ~14% gross return. Lower TER means more return flows to the investor.
